operating_state: Net sales increased 3% to $22.4 billion driven by organic growth across all segments, with Beauty leading at 6% from Personal Care innovation and Hair Care volume increases. Grooming achieved 5% growth from innovation-driven pricing despite competitive pressures. Growth reflected higher pricing of 1%, favorable mix of 1%, and favorable foreign exchange of 1%, with volume neutral across five reportable segments.
cost_structure: Gross margin declined 70 basis points to 51.4% due to unfavorable product mix, product and packaging investments, and higher tariff costs, partially offset by manufacturing productivity savings. SG&A decreased 20 basis points to 25.2% driven by productivity savings in marketing spending, while operating margin compressed 50 basis points to 26.2%. The company incurred restructuring costs across multiple expense categories.
material_events: Company announced comprehensive portfolio and productivity plan targeting $1.5-2.0 billion in restructuring costs over two years, including reduction of up to 7,000 non-manufacturing overhead personnel by fiscal 2027. Completed limited market portfolio restructuring with substantial liquidation of Argentina operations, recording $0.8 billion after-tax restructuring charges primarily from accumulated foreign currency translation losses. Clorox and P&G jointly decided not to renew Glad joint venture agreement, with Clorox purchasing P&G's minority interest for approximately $500 million cash proceeds.
macro_exposures: Company faces heightened tariff cost pressures contributing to gross margin decline, with significant exposures to oil-derived materials like resins and paper-based materials like pulp subject to commodity price volatility. Foreign currency translation reduced comprehensive income by $20 million compared to a $1.0 billion positive impact in the prior year, reflecting strengthening dollar headwinds across operations in approximately 70 countries.
forward_outlook: Management expects to incur half of the restructuring costs by end of fiscal 2026 with remainder in fiscal 2027, targeting improved cost structure through streamlined organization across business units and functions. Company reaffirmed expectation to reduce outstanding shares through approximately $5 billion in direct share repurchases in fiscal 2026 financed by operating cash flows and debt issuance.
uncertainty_profile: The Gillette indefinite-lived intangible asset carrying value of $12.8 billion remains sensitive to discount rate, growth rate, and royalty rate assumptions. Restructuring program execution across business units carries implementation risks over two-year timeline, while pending fair market value negotiations with Clorox for Glad joint venture termination may affect final proceeds timing.
